channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-15
item.description:
The open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `scienceverse/metacheck` repository has enhanced its data processing capabilities with the addition of JASP file processing and improved study categorization. Meanwhile, the `quest-bih/oddpub` project has refined its ontogenerator, improving reuse and reference detection. Connections between projects are also evident, such as the synchronization of `giladfeldman/escicheck` with the `escicheckapp` repository, highlighting the collaborative spirit of the community. Other projects, like `JamieCummins/regcheck` and `giladfeldman/docpluck`, have addressed various issues and made updates to their respective tools, demonstrating the community's commitment to continuous improvement. Overall, the community is celebrating a productive period, with many projects pushing forward and making meaningful contributions to the field.

31 commits across 3 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck, oddpub

Most active:

regcheck25 commits
metacheck3 commits
oddpub3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was worked on, where the ability to process JASP files was added. As stated, "better study categorization" was also implemented in this branch. Additionally, "better scale extraction" was achieved, enhancing the overall functionality of the repository. The changes were made by Lakens, who contributed these specific features to the `data_check` branch on July 15, 2026. The commit focused on enhancing data processing and organization capabilities.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 0a34c42 [data_check] process jasp files, better study categorization, better scale extraction. — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the repository, as indicated by the commit message "Merge branch 'feature-non-apa-newer' of https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck into feature-non-apa-newer". Additionally on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, according to the corresponding commit message. No other branches were mentioned in the provided commit messages, so the work appears to be isolated to `feature-non-apa-newer`. The nature of the "admin updates" is not specified beyond the commit message. No new features or modules beyond "admin updates" were mentioned in the commit messages.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: oddpub: 1 commit today
item.description:
quest-bih/oddpub

The `main` branch was the focus of recent work, with several commits aimed at the ontogenerator. On July 15, the ontogenerator was modified to "improved reuse and upon request, references detection", enhancing its functionality. The day prior, two commits were made to the `main` branch, one of which added "no_new_code statements" to the ontogenerator, and another that involved "debugging open data detection db 2023-2024", targeting specific issues with the database. The ontogenerator received targeted changes to enhance its detection capabilities and address potential issues. Overall, the work on the `main` branch concentrated on refining the ontogenerator's performance and accuracy.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 2b600f3 [main] improved reuse and upon request, references detection — ontogenerator

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
